notes: After a clean exit of session S, agy is relaunched with --continue in the same cwd. LIVE-VERIFIED (recording 2026-06-20): agy --continue appends to the ORIGINAL <conv-id> transcript (no new conv minted), so the SAME daemon session_id reappears across both PID lifetimes — exactly ONE transcript_new for the whole recording, with TWO ready->working->ready turn cycles on that single session row (12:43:04 born ready -> working -> ready for turn 1; 12:43:23 working -> ready for the resumed turn 2). This is the load-bearing assertion and it holds. PID binding is best-effort and NOT asserted, so the clean Ctrl-D exit between the two lifetimes emits NO process_exited (agy is pid=0 — it reports its scratch dir, not the launch cwd, as the run_command Cwd, so DiscoverPID never matches; verified across 1.1/1.2/1.4). The v2 phases pin same_session_as:v1_session_birth so the resumed turn must fire on the SAME session row. Observations omit cost/tokens (no usage in antigravity's JSONL); model harvested from <USER_SETTINGS_CHANGE> reports 'Gemini 3.1 Pro'.

text: First launch: conv-id session appears in ready after the first turn
First user prompt drives ready -> working
First turn completes back to ready (then a clean Ctrl-D exit — no process_exited binds for this pid=0 adapter)
Resume: agy --continue reuses the SAME conv-id session row; the first new turn drives the SAME session ready -> working
Resumed turn completes back to ready on the SAME session row (no new session minted)
